question: Which of the following statements about DMN modeling in Camunda Modeler is NOT correct?

options.text: Modeling starts in the Decision Requirements Diagram (DRD) view.
New DMN elements can be added from the left-side palette or via the context menu of a selected element.
The DMN modeling experience differs significantly between Camunda Desktop Modeler and Camunda Web Modeler.
After morphing a decision into a decision table, you open the table by clicking the overlay on the upper-left corner of the decision.

a.text: True. The docs state that modeling starts in the DRD view, from which elements are added to the canvas.

b.text: True. The docs describe both the left-side palette (drag and drop) and the element context menu as ways to add new DMN elements.

c.text: False, so this is the correct answer for a NOT question. The docs explicitly say Camunda Desktop and Web Modeler both offer the same DMN 1.3 modeling experience.

d.text: True. The docs say editing a decision table begins by clicking the overlay on the upper-left corner of the decision after it has been morphed into a table.

explanation: The DMN modeling docs emphasize that Desktop and Web Modeler share the same DMN 1.3 modeling experience. The other three statements are direct paraphrases of the same docs page.
